language_rules.jargon_level: medium; dev tools and APIs allowed but explained briefly
language_rules.sentence_length: short to medium; punchy and direct
language_rules.use_of_questions: strategic; create curiosity or challenge assumptions
language_rules.code_snippet_style: Use pseudo-JSON, CLI commands, or bullet steps; avoid long code blocks in single tweets

persuasion_techniques: engagement via short questions and open loops
contrast (DIY spaghetti scripts vs maintainable workflows)
future pacing (imagine 10x time saved with one automation)
humor_style: sometimes sarcastic, sharp, never crass; use sparingly
audience_relationship: positioned as a friendly automation architect: we ship workflows you can steal
swearing_policy: avoid; allow mild slang only if authentic and value-led
emoji_usage: sparingly; use to direct attention or emphasize outcomes (👇🔥📈)

formatting_guidelines.headlines: bold, curiosity-driven hooks; use numbers and contrasts
formatting_guidelines.subheadings: clear and sharp; numbered when in threads
formatting_guidelines.bullets: use '-' or '→' for frameworks, steps, and checklists
formatting_guidelines.emphasis: use CAPS for punch; emojis sparingly for direction
formatting_guidelines.line_spacing: multiple short lines for scannability; avoid dense paragraphs
formatting_guidelines.image_usage: use code screenshots, workflow diagrams, and output demos
